description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a rectangular wooden cabinet and a prominent convex glass screen. The front face features a large, rounded display on the left, a black speaker grille below, and a set of three rotary control knobs and a large tuning dial on the right. The wood grain finish covers the top, sides, and back, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance. The front panel is bordered by a thin, dark bezel that frames the screen and controls. The back panel is solid and unadorned, likely housing ventilation and access panels for internal components. This television would typically be found in older urban homes, garages, or as a discarded item on the street.
description: A vintage wooden television with a convex glass screen, rotary knobs, and a speaker grille, featuring a classic mid-century design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wood grain finish and the depth of the cabinet, with no visible controls or screen.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying a flat wooden back panel, with no visible ports or controls.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, highlighting the wood texture and boxy shape.
description_view_3: Front view showing the convex glass screen, three rotary knobs, a large tuning dial, and a black speaker grille.
attribute_bezel_design: Thin, dark, rectangular bezel with a wood grain finish and minimal styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: Three rotary knobs and one large tuning dial arranged vertically on the right front panel.
